items.title: Map incident plotting still not utilizing cross to plot accurately
items.description:
Reporter: J. GARCIA

Date & Time: 04/25/2025, 11:47

Description:
I generated 2 calls, CC26-000035 and 36, both of which had a cross st and plotted on the map blocks away and for the first in another hamlet (primary rd was RT-112).

Where did this occur?
My account, Mac

Steps to Reproduce:
-

Expected Behavior:/
My account, Mac

Actual Behavior::
1st plotted miles away, 2nd was across CR-83 about 1.5 miles or so away

Severity:
Medium (affects workflow)

Frequency:
Always

Device Type:
Desktop

Brower / App:
Safari

Error Messages:


Additional Notes:


Screenshots:

items.title: Feature: Scanner for ID’s
items.description:
Requestor: Jake Prague

Summary:
When adding a person allow an officer to scan ID’s add a way where we can quickly scan them as well. (barcode scan)
Also allow an officer to add a person, not just supervisors / admins
Problem to Solve:
Saves on input time. 

Workflow / Use Case:
All units when obtaining a persons ID. 

Alternate Ideas:

items.title: [BUG]: Status
items.description: When hitting 10-36 (on scene) in the call info box, it changes their status symbol to acknowledge instead of on scene. Their status should change to acknowledge after they acknowledge the pop-up from being assigned to the call.

items.title: [FEATURE]: See if the map can search utilizing the cross street as well to plot a better location
items.description: [FEATURE]: See if the map can search utilizing the cross street as well to plot a better location

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body: How it works now: When a call has a cross street filled in and the address is a street name without a house number (e.g., SUNRISE HWY × HALLOCK AVE), the map now sends Nominatim the query "SUNRISE HWY & HALLOCK AVE, Brookhaven, NY" instead of just "SUNRISE HWY, Brookhaven, NY". Nominatim handles intersection queries well, so the pin drops at the actual intersection rather than somewhere along the full length of the street.

items.title: [BUG]: Memo Book
items.description: sort by ascending or descending times instead of type, possible add a date column

items.title: Emails in wrong time zone
items.description: Emails are in the wrong time zone

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body: Fixed and pushed. The root cause was the same as the PDF bug — Node.js runs in UTC on the server, so any Date method called without an explicit timeZone option uses UTC. Every email (priority alerts, 10-1 alerts, call closed, memo approved/rejected/submitted, password changed, daily/weekly reports) now formats all times through five helper functions that all specify timeZone: 'America/New_York'.

items.title: [BUG]: After an event is created
items.description: After an event is created, if you want to switch the event type, the event list disappears in the drop box

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
In edit mode, the CC number at the top of the event detail becomes an amber-bordered input field — type the correction, hit SAVE, done
Server checks the new number isn't already used by a different event and returns a clear "already in use" error if so; the value is auto-uppercased before saving
The number remains read-only when not editing, same as before

items.title: [FEATURE]: Dispatch log
items.description: On specifically the dispatchers side, could every single one of their entries be tracked on a dispatch log along with manual entried (when we run dmv and stuffl ike that), similar to the paper one they have now to replace it?

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
LOG button in the top bar (dispatcher view only) — click to open the full-screen log panel, click again to return to the normal view

Auto-logged entries for every dispatcher action:

Call created — CC #XXXX CREATED — [type] at [location]
Call closed — CC #XXXX CLOSED
Unit dispatched to a call — STA21 DISPATCHED TO CC #XXXX
Unit cleared from a call — STA21 CLEARED FROM CC #XXXX
Status changes — STA21: AV → DP — CC #XXXX
Check-ins / sign-offs — STA21 CHECKED IN (Vehicle: 123)
Manual entries — free-text input at the bottom of the log panel for DMV runs, phone calls, shift notes, etc.

Color codi...

items.title: Allow users to delete user accounts
items.description: Allow admins to delete user accounts of lower permission then them from admin panel.

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
Delete user: Now soft-deletes (sets account inactive) instead of hard-deleting, which was crashing with 500 because the user is referenced in events, notes, assignments, and other tables. The account disappears from the active user list but historical records stay intact.

Event type dropdown: Removed the isActive filter — the dropdown now shows all event types. The filter was causing the dropdown to appear empty when the current event's type was flagged inactive, or when the load was still in-flight.

Print dispatch log: PRINT LOG button in the top-right of the log panel opens a formatted pri...

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
LOG button in the top bar (dispatcher view only) — click to open the full-screen log panel, click again to return to the normal view

Call created — CC #XXXX CREATED — [type] at [location]
Call closed — CC #XXXX CLOSED
Unit dispatched to a call — STA21 DISPATCHED TO CC #XXXX
Unit cleared from a call — STA21 CLEARED FROM CC #XXXX
Status changes — STA21: AV → DP — CC #XXXX
Check-ins / sign-offs — STA21 CHECKED IN (Vehicle: 123)
Manual entries — free-text input at the bottom of the log panel for DMV runs, phone calls, shift notes, etc.

Color codi...

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
When attempting to delete a user you get this error:
Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 500 (Internal Server Error)

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
Done and pushed. Here's how it works:

UI: A red DELETE button appears in the Actions column only for users the logged-in admin outranks. Clicking it replaces itself with an inline CONFIRM / CANCEL prompt — no accidental deletions. The row disappears from the list immediately on success.

Server enforces the same rules independently:

Cannot delete yourself
Cannot delete a peer (same role) or anyone with a higher role
Returns a clear error message if either check fails
Hard-deletes the row and writes an audit log entry with the deleted user's username and role

items.title: Monitor View
items.description: Can we have the full event type info visible on both monitors? Some event types are more comprehensible then others but showing the full title would help

items.title: CC Update
items.description: If we can make the CC number editable as well on created call, in instance dispatch puts in the wrong CC number.

items.title: [BUG]: Unexpected Token
items.description: After creating a call, could close or change the status. Showed the message "Unexpected token '<'"<html>,h"... is not valid JSON"

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body: The root cause is that the server crashes (unhandled PostgreSQL error) and returns an HTML 500 page instead of JSON — then res.json() fails. This almost certainly started happening after the locationCommonName schema change because the migration hasn't been applied yet, so Drizzle tries to SELECT location_common_name but the column doesn't exist in the actual database.

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body: The "not valid JSON" error means the server returned an HTML page instead of JSON — almost always a crash that isn't caught, or a bad URL. Let me find where close/status-change is called and what the event ID looks like at that point.

items.title: CC on call box info and monitors
items.description: Common name not showing on information box for CC or either event or unit monitor

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
Map marker clipping (second attempt): The previous fix (iconSize: [0, 0]) alone wasn't enough. The label div now uses position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0 so it breaks out of the 0×0 Leaflet container entirely instead of depending on overflow: visible behavior. pointer-events: none is also added so clicks still register on the marker.

Common name: The field was missing end-to-end — it wasn't stored anywhere when a common location was selected.

DB: new location_common_name column, migration 0014
Server: added to schema, event service (list/detail/create/update), route schemas
Client: saved whe...

items.title: [FEATURE]: Make dispatch have the ability to mark units in service
items.description:
### Requester Name / Unit

J. Garcia

### Feature Title

Have dispatch make units go in service

### Summary

Can we make it so dispatch has the ability to mark units in service even if the officer hasn't logged on yet? Example: McCabe not logging in because he doesnt take a tablet. Can they log him on to update his status on the CAD?
Maybe a drop down box when logging in to select a unit, and shows info?

### What problem would this solve?

Units not logging in

### Proposed Solution

See above

### Alternative Ideas

_No response_

### Priority

Low

### Area

UI / UX

### Who would use this...

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
Server (/tours/index.ts):

GET /tours/field-officers — returns active officers/supervisors for the dropdown
GET /tours/all-units — returns all active units grouped by DGroup
POST /tours/manual-checkin — creates a tour for the selected officer, handles conflicts, emits real-time updates, and audit-logs the action
Client:

ManualCheckinModal.tsx — full modal with officer dropdown (with info card), unit dropdown (grouped by DGroup with status indicator), vehicle number, optional mileage/condition/court return date
UnitStatusBoard.tsx — "+ MANUAL CHECK-IN" button in the header, visible to dispatch...

items.title: [FEATURE]: Prevent screen from dimming
items.description:
### Requester Name / Unit

J. Garcia

### Feature Title

Prevent screen from sleeping

### Summary

This is being ran on tablet,s similar to youtube on a webbrowser the screen wont dim / go inactive, is there anything we can do to prevent screen diming when we are on the site?

### What problem would this solve?

Screen diming / timing out

### Proposed Solution

figure it out

### Alternative Ideas

_No response_

### Priority

Low

### Area

UI / UX

### Who would use this and when?

_No response_

### Additional Notes

_No response_

### Confirmation

- [x] I have checked existing issues an...

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
Done. Here's how it works:

useWakeLock hook (src/hooks/useWakeLock.ts):

Calls navigator.wakeLock.request('screen') on mount — this is the standard Web API that tells the browser "don't dim the screen"
Registers a visibilitychange listener: when an officer switches apps and comes back, the lock is automatically re-acquired (browsers release it when the page goes to the background)
Releases cleanly on unmount
Fails silently if the browser doesn't support it (no errors, no broken UI)
TopBar shows a small ☀LOCK label next to the connection dot when the wake lock is active, so you can confirm it'...

Preferences modal — accessible via the ⚙ PREFS button in the top-right (replaces the old ☀ button):

Theme: Dark / Light toggle (same behavior as before, now lives in the panel)
Font Size: Small (14px) / Normal (16px) / Large (18px) / X-Large (20px) — changes the html root font-size so all Tailwind rem-based text classes (text-xs, text-sm, etc.) scale proportionally without touching a single component
Accent Color: Four terminal palettes — Green (default), Amber, Cyan, Ghost — each with separate dark-mode and light-mode variants so the colors remain readable in both themes. Only the structural...

Signature lines for officer and supervisor
Footer with generation timestamp
File is named MemoBook_<UNIT>_<YYYY-MM-DD>.pdf.

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
When a supervisor rejects a memo:

POST /memo/:tourId/reject now emails the officer with the rejection note and a prompt to log in and fix it (sendMemoRejected)
The memo returns to the officer's queue as memoStatus = 'rejected'
Officer correction flow:

On any subsequent login (with or without an active tour), the memo panel loads GET /memo/my-rejected alongside the active/incomplete books
A red "✕ REJECTED MEMO" banner appears at the top of the panel with the tour date and a FIX button
If there's no active tour at all (officer hasn't checked in), the same info appears on the "no active tour" ...

When submitting the memo book for review this should be like the end of tour we want to change this process to submit the memo book, put the unit out of service, and log the unit out for the day. They should no longer be on the board or anything.

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
Server (memo/index.ts) — POST /memo/submit for active (non-incomplete) tours now:

Sets endedAt = now alongside memoStatus = 'submitted' in a single DB update
Inserts a system memo entry: TOUR ENDED — Memo book submitted for review
Emits officer:signed_off to the dispatch room
Checks for ride-alongs on the same unit — if none, calls notificationService.unitOffDuty() to remove the unit from all boards
Audit logs signedOff: true on the submission
The incomplete-tour submission path (for officers completing a force-ended tour) is unchanged.

Client (MemoBookPanel.tsx) — On successful active-tour ...

items.title: Preset Event Priority Levels not transferring
items.description:
Reporter: J. Garcia

Date & Time: Consistent

Description:
There's a preset priority level for each event type. When that event is selected while creating a call, the preset level doesn't carry. It should obviously remain editable by the dispatcher, however in normal call creation that should carry over.

Where did this occur?
Any/every device used, desktop

Steps to Reproduce:
Create an event, select a high priority event type, it doesn't change on the editable screen or when you create it.

Expected Behavior:/
Any/every device used, desktop

Actual Behavior::
It stayed a level 3

Severity:
L...

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body: The bug is on line 279 — the event type onChange only sets eventTypeId, never touches priority. A one-line fix

items.title: Feature: Plot Map
items.description:
Requestor: J. Garcia

Summary:
Able to plot call locations on the map and create a route to it?

Problem to Solve:
More map featues

Workflow / Use Case:


Alternate Ideas:

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
Server — new GET /api/route endpoint (requires auth) proxies to the OSRM public routing API. OSRM uses OpenStreetMap road data and returns a detailed polyline with hundreds of coordinate points following actual streets. The server converts the GeoJSON [lon, lat] coordinates into Leaflet's [lat, lon] format before returning them.

MapView — a useEffect fires whenever the officer's GPS position or their assigned call's geocoded location changes. It calls /api/route, stores the full road geometry in state, and renders it as a solid cyan Polyline. If OSRM is unreachable or returns no route (e.g., ...

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body: Its drawing a line across the map, we need to use roadways

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body: Commit origin/claude/brookhaven-cad-system-NK6yM

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
Call location pins

Every active/pending call is geocoded and plotted as a colored diamond pin
P1 calls = red, P2 = amber, P3+ = gray
The pin label shows the event type code (e.g. 1039/ASSTO), and the popup shows event number, type, priority, status, and address
Routing to assigned call

If an officer is signed in, assigned to a call, and has GPS active — a dashed cyan line is drawn from their pin to the call pin
The assigned call popup shows "★ MY CALL" to make it immediately identifiable
The "Get Directions" link opens Google Maps with the officer's GPS as the starting point; for dispatchers...

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
Geocode event addresses on map open using the existing /api/geocode Nominatim proxy, throttled and cached per address string
Plot event pins colored by priority (P1=red, P2=amber, others=dim) with event number, type, and address in the popup
Route polyline — if an officer is on duty and assigned to a call, draw a dashed green line from their GPS pin to that call's pin
Directions link — event popup opens Google Maps with saddr=officer GPS → daddr=event location when the viewer is an officer with GPS; otherwise just the event location
Fit bounds includes both officer pins and event pins

items.title: Feature: Officer / General View event from unit monitor
items.description:
Requestor: J. Garcia

Summary:
Allow accounts to click on the event from the unit monitor to get details about it, not just from the event monitor.

Problem to Solve:
Allow users to easily view events.

Workflow / Use Case:


Alternate Ideas:

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body: See commit claude/brookhaven-cad-system-NK6yM

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
licking an event number in the unit monitor now opens the full call detail modal. The implementation:

UnitRow — event number cell is a clickable <button> (with stopPropagation) when the unit has an active event; clicking the row itself still opens the status modal as before
DGroupSection — passes onEventClick through to each UnitRow
UnitStatusBoard — tracks selectedEventId locally and renders EventDetailModal when set; canEdit/canNote are derived from the logged-in user's role so officers get read-only view while dispatchers can edit
Works in all tab configurations — units-only, split view, a...

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
UnitRow — make event number cell a clickable button calling onEventClick(eventId)
DGroupSection — pass onEventClick through
UnitStatusBoard — add selectedEventId state, render EventDetailModal

items.title: Feature: Make a tab to view both the event and unit monitor side by side.
items.description:
Requestor: J. Garcia

Summary:
I want a view to see both pages side by side

Problem to Solve:
Allow units to see both.

Workflow / Use Case:


Alternate Ideas:

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
Commit claude/brookhaven-cad-system-NK6yM should fix this issue.

The DispatchPage already has a split-pane layout — the OfficerPage uses tabs and has no equivalent. The fix is to add a SPLIT option to the OfficerPage tab bar that shows events and units simultaneously. Simple one-file change.

Done. A SPLIT tab now appears in the officer page tab bar between UNITS and MAP. Clicking it shows the event board on the left half and the unit board on the right half simultaneously — the same layout the dispatch page uses. Clicking any other tab returns to single-pane view.

items.title: Memo book force logoff
items.description: If we force a unit to sign off, and they dont complete their memo book, make it so that a supervisor knows its incomplete in the review tab, and allow the officer to complete it next time they sign in.

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body: This issue has been completed / fixed.

items.title: Needs assistance
items.description: If someone's status is changed to "need assistance (10-1), maybe an alert tone to all units and dispatch

comments.author: ryanpfister
ryanpfister
comments.body: This should be implemented see commit origin/claude/brookhaven-cad-system-NK6yM

items.title: Pop up notification
items.description: Pop up notification with sound for assigned calls to any officer assigned

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body: This issue has been fixed / implemented.

items.title: Create noted with status's
items.description:
General -
Add the ability to create notes with statuses, mainly when not assigned to a call, so if someone says they are on a personal or taking a 12 (return to town hall) per 104, you can put "PER 104" and its visible on the unit monitor.

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body: This has been complete with commit claude/brookhaven-cad-system-NK6yM

items.title: Change Status
items.description:
General-
Change statuses to Available (green), assigned or dispatched (yellow), enroute (blue), on scene (orange), unavailable (white), 10-18 (meal) (teal), 10-12 (returning to town hall) (brown), out of service (purple), and needs assistance (red)

comments.author: ryanpfister
comments.body:
This has been added to the project.

The commit brookhaven-cad-system-NK6yM solved this issue
